raw_consumer_statement:
Dear Consumer Financial Protection Bureau, I am submitting this complaint because I was deceived by a fraudulent seller and my credit card company has failed to protect me as a consumer, despite clear evidence of fraud. 
In XX/XX/year> and XX/XX/year>, I purchased two separate orders of XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX from XXXX XXXX, which was advertising these items as authentic XXXX XXXX on both its XXXX page and XXXX XXXX. In reality, both purchases turned out to be counterfeit replicas. 
First Purchase ( XX/XX/year> ) Refunded After receiving the first product in XXXX, I had it professionally tested by XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX 
The test, performed on XX/XX/year>, confirmed that the bar was not gold, but instead made of approximately XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX 
( Proof of test results attached. ) Following this evidence, my credit card company issued a refund for the XXXX transaction. 
Second Purchase ( XX/XX/year> ) Not Refunded On XX/XX/year>, I made a second purchase for the same advertised item XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX through the same merchant, XXXX XXXX, for a total of {$3200.00} ( Dispute # XXXX, XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX XXXX CA ). 

When XXXX attempted delivery on XX/XX/year>, I refused the shipment after recognizing the same packaging and merchant details from the prior fraudulent purchase. The item was returned directly to sender ( Refused Return to Sender ).

( Proof of XXXX  tracking attached. ) However, XXXXXXXX does not provide a receipt for refused shipments, and I explained this to my credit card company during the dispute process. Despite this and the supporting documentation, my credit card issuer closed the dispute in favor of the merchant, relying solely on the sellers false claim that I never returned the item and that it remained in my possession. 
This statement is entirely false I never accepted or possessed the merchandise. 

Summary of Issue I was the victim of fraudulent misrepresentation and deceptive business practices. The seller knowingly advertised and sold counterfeit goods, and my credit card company failed to investigate fairly or protect me as a consumer and resolve my dispute. 

While the first fraudulent purchase was refunded, the second purchase from XX/XX/year>, remains unresolved. The merchant has been allowed to retain payment for a product that was both counterfeit and never accepted upon delivery. 
Additionally, I have attached proof from XXXX XXXX website showing that they advertised these products as real gold, not replicas further confirming that the seller knowingly misrepresented their merchandise to consumers.
